constitution.article_ii_agent_authority: PASS - story draft validation performed by @po authority
constitution.article_iii_story_driven: PASS - target story exists in docs/stories
constitution.article_iv_no_invention: BLOCK - story is not self-contained enough and has unresolved source contract ambiguity
constitution.article_v_quality_first: BLOCK - quality gates not run because draft validation blocked before implementation readiness

notes: Community Origin optional section is absent, acceptable for internal story.
CodeRabbit skip notice is present and matches disabled configuration.
executor and quality_gate are present and different.
quality_gate is invalid under validate-next-story executor assignment rules, which list @architect, @dev, and @pm as known quality gate agents.
For code/features/logic work, the expected assignment is executor @dev and quality_gate @architect.
The story also includes UI/accessibility work that can involve @ux-design-expert, but not as the formal quality_gate under the current validation rules.

issues: Scope combines too many independent implementation areas in one story.
Concrete schemas, endpoints, files, event contracts, cache behavior, quality rules, and dependency order are not summarized in the story.
References point mostly to whole documents instead of exact sections/tasks.
Developer must read the full Social PRD/SPEC/tasklist to implement safely.
Acceptance criteria are too broad to verify without external decomposition.
Test files, fixtures, and validation steps are not mapped to specific acceptance criteria.

blocking_issues: Story scope is too large; source tasklist already separates this into SO-V01..SO-V04, SO-D01, SO-C01..SO-C04, SO-A01, and TASK-SO01..TASK-SO18.
Executor assignment is invalid: quality_gate @ux-design-expert is not a known quality gate in validate-next-story rules; code/features/logic maps to @dev with @architect.
Dev Notes are not self-contained enough to satisfy No Invention for implementation.
File locations are broad path groups rather than task-to-file mappings.
Webhook contract ambiguity: DOCUMENTO_GERAL section 11.5 uses event + nested data fields, while TASK-SO15 describes a flat brand_kit_id/updated_at/signature payload.
should_fix_issues: Split or narrow the story into implementable increments.
Map each acceptance criterion to exact source tasks and verification steps.
Add security and integration constraints from DOCUMENTO_GERAL.
Clarify webhook payload/header/signature contract before implementation.
next_action: Return to @sm *draft or @pm to split/refine the story, correct executor quality gate assignment, and resolve the webhook payload contract.
